popularStory.headline: Helmsley Castle, English Heritage site
popularStory.description:
https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/helmsley-castle/

Helmsley Castle remained in the possession of the de Roos family until 1478 when Edmund de Roos sold it to Richard, Duke of Glouceste …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Margaret de Ros於1340年出生在英國越可爾爾姆斯利時，她的父親Robert de Ros31歲，母親Margaret Spencer30歲。她於1355年與Sir Thomas Smythe of Carrington在英國艾可利文哈爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1383年在英國英格蘭艾可利文哈爾享年43歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾薩普頓史爾達林頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Margaret de Ros于1340年出生在英国越可爾爾姆斯利时，她的父亲Robert de Ros31岁，母亲Margaret Spencer30岁。她于1355年与Sir Thomas Smythe of Carrington在英国艾可利文哈爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1383年在英国英格兰艾可利文哈爾享年43岁，埋葬在英国英格兰諾薩普頓史爾達林頓。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Ros
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.

variants.name: Rus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Romanian Simion, Grigore, Doina, Dorel, Ilie, Liviu, Petru, Vasile.

Czech, Slovak, and Romanian (of Slavic origin): ethnic name for a Russian, ultimately derived from an Old Norse term meaning ‘oarsman, rower’ (from the root roths-), a reference to the fact that the Russian state was established by Scandinavians who rowed their ships up rivers inland from the Baltic sea. In some cases this may also be origin of the Slovenian and Croatian surname (see 3 below).
Czech: descriptive nickname from the adjective rusý ‘blond’.

variants.name: Ross
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Margaret de Ros was born in 1340, in Helmsley, Yorkshire, England, her father, Robert de Ros, was 31 and her mother, Margaret Spencer, was 30. She married Sir Thomas Smythe of Carrington in 1355, in Rivenhall, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1383, in Rivenhall, Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 43, and was buried in Dallington, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Margaret de Ros est née en 1340, à Helmsley, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Robert de Ros, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Margaret Spencer, avait 30 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Thomas Smythe of Carrington en 1355, à Rivenhall, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1383, à Rivenhall, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 43 ans, et a été enterrée à Dallington, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady Margaret de Ros 1340 in Helmsley, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert de Ros 31 und ihre Mutter Margaret Spencer 30. Sie hat 1355 in Rivenhall, Essex, England Sir Thomas Smythe of Carrington geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1383 im Alter von 43 Jahren in Rivenhall, Essex, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Dallington, Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Margaret de Ros è nata il nel 1340 nel Helmsley, Yorkshire, England, sua padre, Robert de Ros, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Margaret Spencer, aveva 30 anni. Lei sposato Sir Thomas Smythe of Carrington nel 1355, nel Rivenhall, Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1383, nel Rivenhall, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 43 anni, e fu sepolta a Dallington, Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret de Rosが1340年にイギリスヨークシュアイアヘルムスリーで生まれた時，父Robert de Rosは31歳，母Margaret Spencerは30歳であった。1355年にイギリスエセックスリベンハルでSir Thomas Smythe of Carringtonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1383年にイギリスイギリスエセックスリベンハルで43歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアダリントンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret de Ros(이)가 1340년 영국 요르크실르 헬슬리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert de Ros(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Spencer(은)는 30세였다. 그녀는 1355년 영국 에세스 라이븐홀에서 Sir Thomas Smythe of Carrington(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1383년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 라이븐홀에서 43세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르 댈링턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady Margaret de Ros nasceu em 1340, em Helmsley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert de Ros, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Margaret Spencer, tinha 30 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Thomas Smythe of Carrington em 1355, em Rivenhall, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1383, em Rivenhall, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 43 anos, e foi sepultada em Dallington, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Margaret de Ros родилась в 1340 г. в местности хелмсли, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Robert de Ros было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Margaret Spencer, 30. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Thomas Smythe of Carrington в 1355 г. в местности ривенолл, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1383 г. в местности ривенолл, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 43 лет и была похоронена в местности даллингтон, нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Margaret de Ros nació en 1340, en Helmsley, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert de Ros, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Margaret Spencer, tenía 30 años. Se casó con Sir Thomas Smythe of Carrington en 1355, en Rivenhall, Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1383, en Rivenhall, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 43 años, y fue sepultada en Dallington, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
